title: AFTER ACTION REPORT: Rating-Driven Touch Distribution System

rationale.item: The spread-the-love system was suppressing star player performance. LEAD backs averaged only 2.5 carries per game because ceiling/decay applied uniformly to all players including designated feature backs.
Forced carry percentages were too conservative (35% for balanced offense)
Receivers had no ordinal ranking — just binary STARTER/ROTATION — so target share was a coin flip among starters
Stars on bad teams were held back because spread-the-love forced touches to weaker rotation players

actions.description: LEAD/COMPLEMENT exemption from ceiling/decay — only rotation players managed by spread-the-love
Forced carry percentages increased: LEAD 35→54% (balanced), up to 62% (ground_pound); COMPLEMENT 14→20%
LEAD/COMPLEMENT always injected into carrier pool regardless of play family position lists; rating-based weight multiplier (5x LEAD, 3x COMPLEMENT)
Ordinal receiver ranking (recv_rank 1-5) replacing binary STARTER/ROTATION
Decay curve softened (0.75^→0.82^), soft decay trigger raised 75→85%, ceiling/rotation ceiling raised
game_recv_rank field added to Player dataclass; set in assign_game_roles(); included in player stat output
Team file renames: Union College→Rutgers University, Meredith College→Penn State University

validation_claims.claim: 20-game sample: LEAD avg carries 2.5→6.4, LEAD carry share ~13%→~34%, LEAD avg AP yards ~35→95.5, LEAD 100+ yd games rare→45%, top player avg AP yards ~120→235.7, top player 150+ yd games ~30%→75%
validation_claims.check_method: Run 20-game batch sim and compare LEAD player stats

residual_risks: Total rushing carries per team per game naturally low in Viperball (~20) — star dominance shows in all-purpose yards rather than carry count alone
Receiver recv_rank set pregame and doesn't shift mid-game — a cold #1 receiver keeps getting looks
section_headers_original: AFTER ACTION REPORT: Rating-Driven Touch Distribution System
C. LEAD/COMPLEMENT Always in Carrier Pool
E. Decay Curve Softened (Rotation Players Only)
unmapped_content: Date: 'February 25, 2026' appears only in first line of document. Recv_rank formula: recv_score = hands×1.3 + speed×1.0 + lateral×0.8; rank 1 gets 1.6x weight bonus, declining 0.2 per rank; unranked receivers get 0.08x. Design philosophy: 'ratings dictate volume. A star on a 1-11 team should still put up monster numbers because the rating gap between her and the #5 option naturally funnels touches upward.' generate_game_summary() reconciliation step overwrites team yards from player objects — other stat consumers flow through this path so consistency maintained. Note: team renaming changes (Rutgers, Penn State) are unrelated to the touch distribution work — same session.
parser_notes: Date: 'February 25, 2026' appears in the first document line as scope/context. Confirmed. Team renames (Rutgers/Penn State) in the same commit are noted as unrelated to the main feature.
